answer: Coptic uses a system of prefixed articles and possessives. Definite articles include p- (masculine) and t- (feminine), while ou- marks indefinite nouns. Possessive pronouns are prefixed to nouns (pa- = my, pef- = his, pes- = her, etc.). The genitive relationship is expressed using nte or shortened forms n-/m-. Word order places modifiers and numbers after nouns. The language shows grammatical gender and consonant assimilation in certain contexts.
